symptoms.symptom_name: Hard, fixed, pointed, painless midline epigastric structure (self-palpated)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A hard, fixed, pointed, non-tender midline structure just below the sternum, felt while lying flat, is the classic description of a normal xiphoid process.
A hernia is typically soft and reducible, unlike this hard fixed non-reducible structure.
A bony midline structure is atypical for a gastric tumour, but a new palpable mass cannot be fully characterised without in-person examination.
Absence of constitutional weight loss reduces the probability of an upper-GI malignancy.
No blood in vomit or stool argues strongly against active upper-GI pathology.
Absence of pain argues against an acute intra-abdominal process.

symptoms.symptom_name: Palpitations and tachypnoea coupled to acute fear, no ischaemic features

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Sympathetic arousal (racing heart, fast breathing, sense of doom) locked to the moment of fear is characteristic of an acute panic attack.
No chest pain, no radiation, no exertional trigger — the arousal is psychological, not ischaemic.

symptoms.symptom_name: Tachycardia HR 105 and mild hypertension BP 140/85 (clinician-measured)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Panic-driven autonomic arousal; the measured values are a sealed clinician measurement — the telehealth AI infers arousal from the patient's reported palpitations, not a measured rate.

symptoms.symptom_name: Soft non-tender abdomen; hard midline structure consistent with xiphoid; no palpable masses, hepatomegaly or splenomegaly

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: In-person palpation identifying the structure as the xiphoid with an otherwise normal abdomen confirms the benign nature — sealed clinician finding, not available via chat.

investigation_details.expected_result: No mass; normal xiphoid process and abdominal wall
investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: The patient's reassurance-seeking is unmet and health anxiety persists; a routine ultrasound is a reasonable low-harm way to confirm benignity.

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A normal ultrasound confirms the absence of any pathological mass and provides definitive reassurance.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Routine (non-urgent) abdominal wall / upper abdominal ultrasound
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: A low-harm, routine imaging request appropriately confirms the benign nature of a new self-palpated midline structure and provides the reassurance driving the presentation; it does NOT need to be urgent given the negative red-flag screen.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Emergency CT abdomen / urgent same-day imaging
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Not indicated — there are no red flags and no acute abdomen; ordering urgent/emergency imaging would over-investigate a benign asymptomatic finding and reinforce health anxiety.
The tachycardia is panic-driven with no ischaemic features; a cardiac work-up would be over-investigation and mislabel a panic attack.
